txt: Cotes, George (Cotis, Cottys, or Cootis), fellow of Balliol Coll. 1522, B.A. 27 May, 1522; fellow of Magdalen Coll., M.A. 3 Feb., 1525-6, proctor 1531, B.D. 27 March, 1534, D.D. 10 July, 1536, master of Balliol College 1539-45, rector of Fillingham, co. Lincoln, 1542, and of a moiety of Cotgrave, Notts, 1544, rector of Tachley, Oxon, 1549, canon of Chester 1544, and archdeacon, resigned 1554, bishop 1554, until his death about Dec., 1555. See Ath., ii. 763.

Smithe, John (Smythe), B.A. (? from Balliol Coll.) 19 Dec., 1534; fellow Oriel Coll. 1536-54, from Northants; M.A. 27 March, 1539, B.D. supd. Oct., 1545; perhaps rector of Stanwick 1554-5, and of Thornhaugh, Northants, 1562-81. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Parke, Thomas, B.A. 24 Nov., 1529, M.A. 12 July, 1535, fellow of Balliol Coll. 1538.

Browne, Walter, secular chaplain; B.A. (sup. 23 May) 1520, M.A. 3 Feb., 1525-6, B.D. (sup. 29 Jan.) 1532-3, after 14 years' study, fellow of Balliol Coll. until 1542, one of these names rector of Harston, co. Leicester, 1536, and of Chelwood, Somerset, 1543. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Cosen, Robert (Cosin or Cossyn), B.A. 23 May, 1533, M.A. 7 June, 1537; fellow of Balliol Coll. 1547, perhaps vicar of St. Lawrence Jewry, London, 1545, and canon of St. Paul's 1545, rector of Beckenham, Kent, 1547, rector of Crick, Northants, 1548, treasurer of St. Paul's 1558, and vicar of Frampton, Dorset, 1559. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Higginson, Alan (Hickynsonne), B.A. 18 Jan., 1552-3, as "Higgins"; fellow of Balliol Coll. 1557-60, M.A. 1 July, 1557.
Wood, Robert (Wodde), B.A. 29 Nov., 1553; fellow Balliol Coll. 1557-8, M.A. 1 July, 1557.
Shaghnes, Richard (Shafnes), B.A. 14 Nov., 1555, fellow Balliol Coll. 1558-64, M.A. 28 May, 1560, as "Shastenes." See Ath. i. 385; & Bloxam, ii. 39.
Graswith, Arthur (Greswith or Gracewithe), B.A. (sup. May), 1556; fellow of Balliol Coll. 1558-63, M.A. 28 May, 1560.
Radcliff, Edmund (Ratcliffe), B.A. 15 Dec., 1553; fellow Balliol Coll. 1558-60.

Squire, Adam (Squier or Squyer), fellow Balliol Coll. 1560-8, B.A. 6 July, 1560, M.A. 16 Dec., 1564, proctor 1567, master of his college 1571-80, B.D. 10 April, 1575, D.D. 10 April, 1576; vicar of Cumnor, Berks, 1568, rector of Wollaston, Notts, 1570, of Winterbourne Earls, Wilts, 1571, of Fillingham, co. Lincoln, 1572, and of Drayton, Oxon, 1576, canon of St. Paul's 1577, rector of Longworth, Berks, 1578, canon of Lichfield 1586, archdeacon of Middlesex 1557, until his death before Oct., 1588; perhaps father of John 1605. See Fasti, i. 202; Lansdowne MS. 982, f. 91; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Barker, Roger, B.A. 20 Oct., 1558, fellow of Balliol Coll. 1560, M.A. 12 July, 1567, one of these names vicar of St. Laurence Jewry, London, 1572, and of Newbold on Avon, co. Warwick, 1575. See Le Neve, i. 637.
Atkinson, John, B.A. 6 July 1560; fellow of Balliol Coll. 1560-9, M.A. 16 Dec., 1564, inventory (at Oxford) 7 Nov., 1570.
Hammond, Robert (or Hammone), B.A. 8 May, 1562, fellow of Balliol Coll. 1564-70, M.A. 16 Dec., 1564.
Lee, John (Leye or Lye), B.A. 29 April, 1562, fellow of Balliol Coll. 1564.

Coventry, (Sir) Thomas, B.A. 2 June, 1565; fellow Balliol Coll. 1566, of the Inner Temple 1568, as of Bewdley, co. Worcester (2nd son of Richard, of Cassington, Oxon), bencher 1591, autumn reader 1593, serjt.-at-law 1603, king's serjeant 1605, a justice of Common Pleas, and knighted 13 Jan., 1606, died 12 Dec., 1606, father of the next named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.; Fasti, i. 167; Foss, 193; & D.N.B.
Tuncke, John (Tunkes or Tunckis), B.A. 2 June, 1565, fellow Balliol Coll. 1566-73, M.A. 12 June, 1570.

Hill, Adam, fellow Balliol Coll. 1568-72; B.A. 5 April, 1568, M.A. 5 July, 1572, B. & D.D. 2 July, 1591; vicar of Westbury, Wilts, 1577, rector of Gussage St. Nicholas, Dorset, 1579, rector of Studland, Dorset, 1580, of Bishop's Lavington, Wilts, 1580, and of Ebbesborne Wake, Wilts, 1586, canon and succentor of Salisbury 1583, until his death 16 Feb., 1594, buried in the cathedral church. See Ath. i. 623.

Parsons, Robert, from Nether Stowey, Somerset; fellow of Balliol Coll. 1568-74, B.A. 31 May, 1568, M.A. 2 Dec., 1572, admitted to the society of Jesus at Rome 1575, rector of the English college there 1587; died at Rome 5 or 15 April, 1610. See Lansdowne MS. 983, f. 165; & Ath. ii. 63.
Wilson, John, B.A. 13 May, 1566, fellow Balliol Coll. 1569-76, M.A. 16 Dec., 1570; one of these names beneficed in Somerset. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Hyde, Thomas (Hide), B.A. 10 Nov., 1568, fellow of Balliol Coll. 1570-76, M.A. 2 Dec., 1572, B.D. 12 July, 1576, sup. for licence to preach 9 Dec., 1578, D.D. 2 July, 1591; rector of Cranford, Middlesex, 1575, vicar of Marston St. Laurence, Northants, 1575, rector of Chilton Foliatt, Wilts, 1576, of Saddington, co. Leicester, 1578-9, of West Kington, Wilts, 1579, of Boscombe, Wilts, 1596, and of Stratford Tony, Wilts, 1614-18, canon and chancellor of Sarum cathedral 1588, until his death Nov., 1618. See Lansdowne MS. 983, f. 319; Fasti, i. 255; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Stancliff, James, fellow Balliol Coll. 1570-5, B.A. 2 Nov., 1570, M.A. 21 June, 1575; rector of St. Bartholomew-the-Great, London, 1581. See Ath. ii. 65.

Bagshaw, Christopher, fellow of Balliol Coll. 1572, B.A. 12 July, 1572, M.A. 21 June, 1575, after s.s.t.p. (Parisius), principal of Worcester College 1579-81, canon of Lichfield 1578, a Jesuit, committed prisoner to Wisbech Castle, co. Cambridge, 1593. See Ath., ii. 65, 67, 389.

Thornley, Hugh, fellow Balliol Coll. 1572-78, B.A. 26 Nov., 1572, M.A. 21 June, 1575; clerk, licenced 10 June, 1585, to marry Johanna Pecke, of London; perhaps rector of Stanford Dingley, Berks, 1578. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.
Crane, Robert, B.A. 7 Feb., 1571-2; fellow Balliol Coll. 1572-83, M.A. 21 June, 1575, proctor 1580 and 1581, principal New Inn Hall 1586-93. See O.H.S., xii. 12.
Bingley, Peter, B.A. 26 Jan., 1569-70; fellow Balliol Coll. 1572-3, M.A. 2 May, 1573, rector of Braybrooke, Northants, 1572. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Turner, George, fellow Balliol Coll. 1572-82, B.A. 27 March, 1572, M.A. 21 June, 1575; died 1582.

Holland, Thomas, B.A. 9 Dec., 1570, chaplain, fellow of Balliol Coll. 19 Jan., 1573, M.A. 21 June, 1575, sup. for licence to preach 14 March, 1578-9, B.D. 13 July, 1582, D.D. 15 July, 1584, regius professor of divinity 1589-1612, entered of Gray's Inn 2 Feb., 1609-10. Wood states that he was born at Ludlow (perhaps a student of Middle Temple 1571 as 2nd son of Wm., of Burwarton, Salop, gent.), canon of Salisbury 1590, rector of Rotherfield Grays, Oxon, 1591, and rector of Exeter Coll. 1592 until his death 17 March, 1611; buried in St. Mary's church, Oxon; will at Oxford proved 20 April, 1612; father of Wm. 1611. See Lansdowne MS. 983, f. 205; Ath. ii. 111; Boase, 50; Foster's Inns of Court Reg.; Foster's Index Eccl.; & D.N.B.

Whitmore, Francis, scholar Balliol Coll. 1573, B.A. 18 June, 1575, fellow 1576-81, M.A. 9 July, 1579; (2s. Thomas of Madeley), rector of Bingham, Notts, 1581, and of Kirby-Wiske, Yorks, 1582, vicar of Chisledon, Wilts, 1584, canon of Southwell, 1589-98; will dated 10 March, 1597, proved 11 May, 1598; brother of Peter. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Langley, Richard, scholar of Balliol Coll. 1573; B.A. from University Coll. 18 March, 1573-4. See Foster's Index Eccl. & O.H.S. xii. 41.

Kerry, Francis (Keyrie), of Salop, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 17; B.A. 15 July, 1580, M.A. 20 March, 1582-3, B.D. 19 Feb., 1596-7, D.D. 13 June, 1610, vicar of Farringdon, Berks, 1588, and served Burford (1st portion), Salop, 1590, canon of Hereford 1598, treasurer of Hereford cathedral 1606, vicar of Madley, co. Hereford, 1607, rector of Eastham, etc., co. Worcester, 1607, and of Hampton Bishop. co. Hereford, 1615; father of Richard 1616. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Yates, Peter, of co. Lancaster, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 26, entered as "minister pleb. fil.," B.A. 20 June, 1576, M.A. 16 May, 1579 (as Yate); rector of East or Little Shefford 1575, of Appleton 1576, and of Besselsleigh, (all) Berks, 1578. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Dale, George, of Somerset, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 1575, aged 14, B.A. 20 June, 1576; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1578, M.A. 12 April, 1581, proctor 1587, B. & D.C.L. 14 July, 1590; principal of St. Mary Hall about 1587-91, died 25 Nov., 1625. See William, same date.

Southcote, George (Sowthcote), arm. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, student of Inner Temple 1575, as of Shillingford, Devon (s. John); perhaps M.P. Plympton 1597-8, and knighted 23 July, 1603; died in 1631. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Dale, William, of Somerset, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 1575, aged 18; of the Middle Temple 1577, as late of Lyons Inn, gent., son and heir of Henry, of Yebton, Somerset; see George same date. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Ince, Hugh, of Cheshire, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 20; scholar 1575, B.A. 13 Feb., 1579-80, rector of Chipping Ongar 1586, and of Greenstead-juxta-Ongar, Essex, 1593. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Beauforest, Luke, of Oxon, gent. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 1575, aged 41; a student of the Middle Temple 1552, as son of Richard, of Dorchester, Oxon. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Ath., i. xxxvii.

Howe, John, gen. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575 ("entry erased in Mat. Reg."); possibly a student of Middle Temple 1574, as son and heir of John, of London. See Foster's Inns of Court Register.

Hussie, John, gen. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 19; possibly a student of Middle Temple 1582, as son and heir of Richard, of Hawkersbury, co. Hereford. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Barker, Roland, of Salop, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 1575, aged 26; of Haghmonde, Salop, sheriff 1585 (son of James, of Haghmond), father of John 1596, and of Walter 1599.
Stumpe, John, of Wilts, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 16; student of Inner Temple 1579, as of Malmesbury (Wilts); M.P. 1584-5. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Goringe, Edward, of Sussex. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 18; of Oakhurst, Sussex (s. Sir Henry, of Burton, Sussex, kt.), high sheriff Surrey and Sussex 1613.
Deringe, Henry, (s. Thomas), of Southants. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 1575, aged 18; father of Richard the organist, 1610, and brother of Edward same date.
Beresford, Roland (Beriforde), of London, gent. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 1575, aged 18; a student of Inner Temple 1577. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Littleton, Francis, of co. Stafford, eq. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 16, of Melsho, Salop (s. Sir Edward); brother of James same date.
Farre, Richard, of Salop. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 15; one of these names M.P. Ludlow 1584-5. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Pilcher, Thomas, of Sussex, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 18; scholar 1575, fellow 1576, B.A. 28 May, 1576, M.A. 16 May, 1579.
Deringe, Edward, of Southants. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 1575, aged 17; slain in Ireland, brother of Henry same date.
Littleton, James, of co. Stafford, eq. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 17; brother of Francis 1575.
Kirkham, John, of Surrey, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 22; but B.A. 29 March, 1574.
Todde, Abraham, of Newcastle, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 19, B.A. 4 Dec., 1577.
Lee, Edward, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, as Edward; B.A. 27 Feb., 1575-6, as Edmund.
Deringe, Edward, of Sussex. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 1575, aged 16. See Berry's Sussex, 241.
Lenthall, William (Lentall), of co. Hereford, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 19.
Wenham, Thomas (Waynam), of Sussex, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 14.
Stringer, George, of co. Stafford, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 24.
Cooper, Henry (Coopar), of Berks, gent. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 1575, aged 18.
Wordell, Edward, of co. Stafford, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 18.
Leighe, Edmund, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, B.A. 27 Feb., 1575-6.
Warde, Oliver, of co. Derby, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 20.
Heighte, William, of Kent, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 18.
Taylboys, John, of London, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 17.
Brookes, Poynings, of Cheshire. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 1575, aged 20.
Adice, Edward, of Salop, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 1575, aged 22.
Rosewell, John, of Balliol Coll. pleb.; matric. entry under date 1575, as Rowswell.
Leedes, Edward, of Sussex. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 19.
Goldsmithe, John, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 17.
Foster, Edward (Fostar), gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575.
Foster, Robert (Fostar), gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575.
Erthe, Joseph (Earthe), gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575.
Jo(h)nson, Francis, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575.
Philips, Thomas, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 1575.
Hill, Martin (Hyll), fellow Balliol Coll. 1576, B.A. 28 Feb., 1575-6, M.A. 22 June, 1579, rector of Asfordby, co. Leicester, 1580. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Abbot, Robert, of Surrey, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17; B.A. 16 May, 1579, fellow 1581-8, M.A. 7 Feb., 1582-3, sup. for licence to preach 21 Jan., 1586-7, B.D. 4 March, 1593-4, D.D. 22 Nov., 1596, master 1609-17, regius professor of divinity 1612-15, rector of Bishops Hatfield, Herts, 1584, All Saints, Worcester, 1589, and of Bingham, Notts, 1598, chaplain to James I., preb. of Southwell 1610, rector of Fillingham, co. Lincoln, 1611 (of Gray's Inn 1612), bishop of Salisbury 1615, until his death 2 March, 1617-18, eldest son of Maurice Abbot, of Guildford, Surrey, sherman. See Ath., ii. 224, 859; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Wick(h)am, Edward, of Oxon, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16, exhibitioner 1578, B.A. 26 Jan., 1580-1, M.A. 13 Dec., 1583 (incorporated at Cambridge 1587), B. and D.D. 25 June, 1608; rector of Horton, Bucks, 1591, canon of Lincoln 1593 and of Chichester 1603, archdeacon of Dorset 1607, died 21 Aug., 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Henshaw, Edward (Henshall), of Cheshire, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 20; B.A. 2 July, 1580; perhaps vicar of Brenchley 1594, and of Sutton Valence, Kent, 1615; father of Edward Henshaw, of Magdalen Hall, 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Carre, Robert, of co. Lincoln, arm. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18; perhaps of Gray's Inn 1580, son of George, of Sleaford, co. Lincoln, and died 24 Feb., 1592-3.
Franck, Thomas, of co. Stafford, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18; B.A. 11 May, 1583.
Collingwood, Cuthbert (Colingwoode), of Northants, gent. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 19.
Hill, Myles (Aemilius), of co. Stafford, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18.
Lloyde, Jenkin, of co. Montgomery, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17.
Audience, Richard, of Middlesex, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 14.
Yonge, William, of co. Stafford, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18.
Pye, Thomas, of co. Stafford, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18.
Storye, Walter, of Salop, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16.
Essex, William, of Berks, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 19.
Hill, Richard, of Bucks, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16.

Parry, Henry, of Flints, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 18; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 14 Jan., 1579-80, M.A. 23 March, 1582-3, B.D. from Jesus Coll. 6 June, 1597; rector of Llandegla, co. Denbigh, 1596-8, of Rhoscolyn 1601, and of Trefdraeth, Anglesey, 1607, vicar of Llanrhaidr-yn-Mochnant, co. Denbigh, 1612-16, canon of Bangor 1613, rector of Llanfachraeth in Anglesey 1614, until his death in 1617; see Robert 1582. See Ath. i. 666; Thomas, St. Asaph, 730; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Ellis, Clement, of Yorks, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 17, B.A. 31 Oct., 1582; M.A. from Lincoln Coll. 7 July, 1585, rector of Withycombe, Devon, 1591. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Ashby, William, of co. Leicester, gent. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 17; of Loseby Hall, co. Leic., admitted to the Inner Temple 1576. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Bayly, John, of Notts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 March, 1578-9, aged 18; B.A. 31 May, 1582, M.A. 4 Feb., 1584-5.
Cook, John, of London, gent. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 23; B.A. 17 June, 1579.
Nicolas, Anthony, of Wilts, gen. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1578, aged 30; B.A. 5 March, 1577-8.
Brick, Walter (Bryck), of co. Stafford, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 18.
Johnes, William, of Middlesex, gen. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 18.
Bevan, John (Beven), of Salop, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date circa 1578, aged 23.

Hughes, Cadwalader, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 23 Sept., 1580, aged 17; B.A. 14 June, 1585, M.A. 4 July, 1588; B.D. from Jesus Coll. 6 June, 1597, rector off Heathfield 1591, vicar of Bridgewater 1593, and of Skilgate (all Somerset) 1595, archdeacon of Llandaff 1601, vicar of Milverton, Somerset, 1610. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Abbotte, George, of Surrey, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 18; B.A. 31 May, 1582, M.A. 17 Dec., 1585, sup. for licence to preach 2 March, 1593-4, B.D. 4 March, 1593-4, D.D. 9 May, 1597, master of University College 1597-1610, vice-chancellor 1600-3-5, was mainly instrumental in establishing Pembroke College, out of Broadgates Hall in 1624, one of the translators of the New Testament, was never beneficed, dean of Winchester 1600, bishop of Lichfield & Coventry 1609, and of London 1610 (of the Inner Temple 1611), archbishop of Canterbury 1611, until his death 4 Aug., 1633, aged 71, son of Mauriee Abbot, of Guildford, Surrey, sherman. See Ath., ii. 561, 882; & Cole's MS., 5862, 21.

Sanforde, John, of Somerset, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date circa 16 Oct., 1581, aged 16; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 17 Dec., 1586, chaplain 1593-1616, M.A. 27 May, 1595; (s. Richard, of Chard), author of a Spanish grammar, domestic chaplain to Dr. Abbot, archbishop of Canterbury, rector of Guildford Holy Trinity 1593, and Ivychurch, Kent, 1615, and canon of Canterbury (about) 1615, until his death 24 Sept., 1629. See Ath. ii. 471; Bloxam, ii. 129; Lansdowne MS. 984, f. 120; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Davenporte, (Sir) Humphrey, of Cheshire, arm. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1589, and bencher 1613 (as son of William, of Bramhall, Cheshire, esq.), serjt.-at-law 1623, knighted at Greenwich 17 June, 1624, king's serjeant 1625, M.P. Brackley 1588-9, a judge of common pleas 2 Feb., 1630, lord chief baron 1631-44, buried at Macclesfield 4 March, 1644-5, father of the next named. See Foster's Judges and Barristers; Ath., iii. 182; & D.N.B.

Thelwall, Simon (Theolelphe), of co. Denbigh, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date circa 16 Oct., 1581, aged 20; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 28 Feb., 1583-4; of Woodford, Essex, student of Lincoln's Inn 1591 (7s. John, of Batharvan Park, co. Denbigh), chief clerk to Sir Daniel Dunne, D.C.L., judge of the prerogative court; a proctor of the court of arches, and registrar of Bangor, and perhaps M.P. co. Denbigh, 1593-1614; father of William 1628. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Monson, (Sir) William, of co. Lincoln, arm. fil.; Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 14; created M.A. 9 July, 1594; of Kinnersley, Surrey, student of Gray's Inn 1594 (as 2nd son of John, of co. Lincoln, kt.), admiral of the fleet, knighted at the sacking of Cadiz 1596, M.P. Malmesbury 1601, and Reigate 1626; died in Feb., 1642-3, buried in St. Martin's-in-the-Fields. See Ath. ii. 3; & Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Kemyshe, Laurence, of Wilts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 17, fellow 1582-91, B.A. 16 Dec., 1583, M.A. 10 June, 1586; made a voyage to Guiana in America 1595, and also in 1617, with Sir Walter Raleigh, ostensibly to take possession of a golden mine, but being unsuccessful he killed himself Midsummer, 1618. See Ath. ii. 230.

Hawley, John (Haulye), of Middlesex, arm, Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 15; fellow St. John's Coll. 1583, B.C.L. 1588, D.C.L. 13 Oct., 1614, principal of Gloucester Hall 1593-1626; 2s. of Jeremy, of Boston, near Brentford; will at Oxford proved 6 June, 1626; brother of Henry same date. See Fasti, i. 357.

Saunder, (Sir) Nicholas, of Surrey, arm. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 18; student of Inner Temple 1583, as of Ewell, Surrey (s. Nicholas), M.P. Penryn 1588-9, Haslemere and St. Ives 1593, Helston 1597-8, Lostwithiel 1601, Gatton 1604-11, Winchelsea 1626. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Barefielde, Lewis, of London, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 18; fellow of Exeter Coll. 1583-92, B.A. 6 July, 1586, M.A. 28 June, 1589, vicar of Heston, Middlesex, 1591, and of Great Dunmow, Essex, 1594. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Boase, 48.

Whitmore, Peter, of co. Stafford, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date circa 16 Oct., 1581, aged 18, B.A. 9 Feb., 1584-5; (s. Thomas) vicar of Orston, Notts, 1589; will dated 16 Jan., 1608, proved 9 Dec., 1614; brother of Francis. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Shuckburgh, Simon, of co. Warwick, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date circa 16 Oct., 1581, aged 15; of Napton, student of Lincoln's Inn 1583, 3rd son of Anthony, of Shuckburgh; brother of John 1581, and perhaps of the next. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Shuckburgh, John, of co. Warwick, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date circa 16 Oct., 1581, aged 18; of Shuckburgh, co. Warwick, and sheriff; student of Lincoln's Inn 1583; died 20 March, 1631-2; brother of Simon 1581. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Davenporte, Alexander, of Cheshire, arm. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 17; of Barnard's Inn, admitted to Gray's Inn 1585 (as son of William, of Bramhall, Cheshire), buried at Stockport 26 Oct., 1612. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Browne, William, of Sussex, arm. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 17; B.A. 9 Feb., 1584-5, perhaps a student of the Inner Temple 1589, as of Chichester, Sussex, late of Clifford's Inn, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Ive, Mark, of Middlesex, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 19; a student of Inner Temple 1585, as of London; one Mark Ive, of Essex, knighted 23 July, 1603; brother of the next-named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Hanslepp, Robert (Hanslappe), of co. Warwick, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 17; a student of Gray's Inn 1583, as son of John, of Solihull, co. Warwick, esq. See Foster's Gray's Inn Register.

Cliff, Gabriel (Clyff), of co. Worcester, gent. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 17; B.A. 7 Feb., 1583-4, M.A. 6 July, 1587, rector of Ipsley, co. Warwick, 1589. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Oglethorpe, Edward (Ogelthroppe), of Oxon, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date circa 16 Oct., 1581, aged 14; student of Inner Temple 1585, as of Newington, Oxon (1s. Sir Owen O.) See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Robinson, Jasper, of Middx., pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 17; B.A. supd. 7 April, 1584, vicar of Stratton 1599, and rector of Marhamchurch, Cornwall, 1604. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Nevel, Henry, of Somerset, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 19; of Bedminster and Bathwick, Somerset (4th son of Edward, 5th Baron Abergavenny in 1587); father of George 1631.
Hawley, Henry (Haulye), of Middlesex, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 12; 3rd son of Jeremy, of Boston, near Brentford; a merchant in London; brother of John same date.
Ive, William, of Middlesex, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 16; student of Inner Temple 1584, as of London; brother of the last-named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Harveye, William, of Northants, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 25 Nov., 1581, aged 11; possibly 2s. Stephen, of Cotton in Hardingstone, Northants; died 20 Jan., 1633-4, father of Francis 1627.
Gower, Abel (Gowre), of co. Worcester, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 14, B.A. 9 Feb., 1584-5; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1585, M.A. 11 June, 1588, proctor 1596.
Burdett, John, of co. Warwick, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 19 March, 1581-2, aged 15; possibly 3rd son of Thomas Burdett, of Bramcott, and brother of Robert 1576.
Braxton, John, of Hants, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 17; B.A. 7 Feb., 1583-4, M.A. 22 June, 1586, incorp. at Cambridge 1587.
Prynne, William, of London, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date circa 16 Oct., 1581, aged 17; B.A. 17 June, 1585, M.A. 7 May, 1588, B.D. 7 June, 1608.
Blounte, Richard, of co. Leicester, arm. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 16 (? B.A. from Balliol Coll. 29 Nov., 1582).
Higges, George, of co. Stafford, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 17; B.A. 22 June, 1583, M.A. 20 June, 1599.
Hooper, George, of Wilts, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 20; B.A. 31 Jan., 1587-8, M.A. 24 June, 1591.
Pymme, Edward, of Oxon, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 18; B.A. 7 Feb., 1583-4, M.A. 10 June, 1586.
Joy, Thomas, of co. Warwick, gen. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 18; sup. for B.A. 7 May, 1583.
Browne, Francis, of Sussex, arm. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 16; B.A. 9 Feb., 1584-5.
Cressette, Francis, of co. Salop, arm. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date circa 16 Oct., 1581, aged 15.
Bermore, James, of London, gent. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date circa. 16 Oct., 1581, aged 18.
Worthe, Anthony, of co. Warwick, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 16.
Butler, Anthony, of co. Lincoln, arm. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 16.
Butler, William, of co. Lincoln, arm. Balliol Coll., matric.-entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 18.
Lygon, Hugh, of co. Gloucester, gen. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 16.
Fleminge, John, of Yorks, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 17.
Poole, Robert, of Salop, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 15.
Reeve, Robert, of Wilts, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 May, 1581, aged 15.
Mo(o)re, Richard, of Dorset, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry 25 Nov., 1581, aged 11.
Guy, Joseph (Gye), of Oxon, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 19 March, 1581-2, aged 32.

Parry, Robert, of Flints, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1582, aged 19, B.A. from St. Mary Hall 28 June, 1588, M.A. from Christ Church 2 March, 1593-4; one of these names rector of Llanfair Pwllywingyll, co. Anglesey, 1603; see Henry 1579. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Tooker, Charles, of Wilts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 Feb., 1582-3, aged 17; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 28 Nov., 1586; of Lincoln's Inn and of Abingdon, Berks (3s. Charles, of Maddington, Wilts); father of the next-named.

Heylyn, Thomas (Helin), of Salop, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 18 May, 1582, aged 18; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 29 Nov., 1586, M.A. 5 June, 1589, then in orders, as Heylyn. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.
Cuddington, John, of Surrey, pleb. Balliol Coll. matric. 28 July, 1582, aged 23; B.A. 14 Dec., 1590, M.A. 5 July, 1594, of Lincoln College, will and admon. bond (at Oxford) 24 April, 1613.
Acton, John, of Salop, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 14 June 1582, aged 28; (? M.P. Droitwich 1597-8, and probably 5th son of William Acton, M.P.) See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Goode, John, of London, gent. Balliol Coll. matric. 6 July, 1582, aged 11; student of Lincoln's Inn 1587 (as of Middlesex), M.P. Camelford 1604-11. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Broughton, Edward, of Salop, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 22 March, 1582-3, aged 17; bar.-atlaw, Lincoln's Inn, 1597, sometime of Thavies Inn. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Willoughbey, Edward, of co. Lincoln, baronis fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 2 July, 1582, aged 17; 3rd son of Charles, baron Willoughby of Parham.
Broughton, Peter, of co. Stafford, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 18 May, 1582, aged 17; probably 5th son of Thomas, of Broughton, co. Stafford.
Batman, Robert, of London, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 6 April, 1582, aged 17; B.A. from St. John's Coll. 14 June, 1585.
Hills, Richard, of London, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 27 July, 1582, aged 12; B.A. 10 Feb., 1586-7.
Foster, Godard, of Sussex, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 July, 1582, aged 18, (of Iden, Sussex).
Austine, Ceasar (or Augustinus), of London, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 14 Sept., 1582, aged 15.
Millward, Richard (Myllward), of Salop, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 28 July, 1582, aged 26.
Floyd, John, of co. Denbigh, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 31 Aug., 1582, aged 16, as Lhuid.
Holland, Hugh, of co. Denbigh, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 March, 1582-3, aged 24.
Brinsley, Gervis, of Notts, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 16 Nov., 1582, aged 16.
Frauncis, Edmond, of Berks, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 14 June, 1582, aged 14.
Hills, Humphrey, of London, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 27 July, 1582, aged 14.
Poole, Robert, of Notts, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 16 Nov., 1582, aged 17.

Kemys, Nathaniel (Kemis) of Wilts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 19 July, 1583, aged 17; demy Magdalen Coll., 1586-9, B.A. 27 June, 1587, fellow 1589-1605, M.A. 5 May, 1592, licenced to preach (to practice medicine, in register) 17 July, 1598, B.D. 14 Dec., 1600, incorporated at Cambridge 1604, rector of Broughton, Hunts, 1601, and of Crick, Northants, 1610. See Bloxam, iv. 226; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Hendene, (Sir) Edward, of Kent, Balliol Coll., matric. 21 June, 1583, aged 15 (as gent.); bar.-atlaw, Gray's Inn, 1591, bencher 1614 (as of Benenden, Kent), M.P. Rye 1614, serjeant-at-law 1616, a baron of the exchequer 1639, and knighted; died Feb., 1643-4. See Foss' Judges.

Holland, George, of Salop, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 31 Jan., 1583-4, aged 20; perhaps B.A. 12 Nov., 1588, fellow, M.A. 8 June, 1591, see also next entry; possibly student of Lincoln's Inn 1593, as of Salop; will at Oxford 30 June, 1596. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Wellinch, Humphrey, of Berks, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1583, aged 18, B.A. 26 May, 1587, M.A. 6 July, 1590 (as Wellinge or Willing); student of Gray's Inn 1591, as Wellinge. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Hill, Edward, of Surrey, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 26 April, 1583, aged 17; (B.A. 10 Feb., 1586-7); M.A. from Gloucester Hall 23 May, 1590; one of these names beneficed in Somersetshire. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Sandes, John, of Surrey, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 26 April, 1583, aged 18; son and heir of John, of Pattensham, alias Randolfs, parish of Letherhead, Surrey; brother of Robert 1584, and father of Thomas 1617.
Sandes, Robert, of Surrey, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 6 March, 1583-4, aged 18; of Fetcham, Surrey (2s. John, of Pattensham, alias Randolfs, parish of Leatherhead, Surrey); brother of John 1583.
Griffith, William (Gruffyn), of co. Merioneth, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 14 Sept., 1583, aged 17, B.A. from St. Mary Hall 21 Feb., 1586-7; perhaps M.A. from Balliol Coll. 27 June, 1590.
Evans, William, of co. Hereford, "Verbi minister." Balliol Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1583, aged 30; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 12 Feb., 1585-6; M.A. from Jesus Coll. 21 June, 1589.
Saunder, Henry, of Surrey, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. entry 30 Aug., 1583, aged 14; student of Inner Temple 1590, as of Ewell, Surrey. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Bradshaw, William (Bradshowe), of Bucks, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 6 March, 1583-4, aged 16; B.A. 23 Jan., 1587-8, M.A. 19 Feb., 1590-1.
Turner, John, of London, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1583, aged 17, B.A. 16 Feb., 1586-7.
Snowe, Walter, of Wilts, "gen. cond." Balliol Coll., matric. 19 July, 1583, aged 20.
Goddard, John, of Berks, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 6 March, 1583-4, aged 16.
Turner, James, of London, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1583, aged 16.
Davie, John, of Salop, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 11 Oct., 1583, aged 17.
Nevell, Philip, of Yorks, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 Nov 1583, aged 17.

Turner, W(illiam), of London, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry 16 Oct., 1584, aged 14; M.A. from Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1589 or 1590; incorporated 30 April, 1602; having been 10 years a student in foreign academies, was admitted B.Med. from Balliol and licenced to practice 13 July, 1604, D.Med. 1 June, 1608; incorporated at Cambridge 1609; lived in Fetter Lane. See Lansdowne MS. 981, f. 64; & Fasti, i. 326.

Smith, Leonard (Smyth), of co. Hereford, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1584, aged 18; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 2 July, 1588; perhaps rector of Stockerston, co. Leicester, 1593. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Moris, Evan, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 5 June, 1584, aged 20; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 16 Feb., 1587-8, M.A. 18 May, 1591; perhaps cursal canon of St. Asaph, 1626. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Gilbart, Ambrose, of Wilts., pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 22 Jan., 1584-5, aged 18; B.A. 6 July, 1586, M.A. 21 April, 1589, rector of Hampreston, Dorset, 1591. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Remington, Francis, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 30 Oct. 1584, aged 17; perhaps rector of Broxholme, 1592, and canon of Lincoln, 1592. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wynn, William (Winn), of co. Denbigh, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 3 April, 1584, aged 16; student of Gray's Inn 1587, as of Gresford, co. Denbigh. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Gun, William, of Wilts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 22 Jan., 1584-5, aged 17 (and query again 5 Nov., 1585, aged 19); B.A. 9 Feb., 1587-8, M.A. 24 June, 1591.
Singleton, John, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 30 Oct., 1584, aged 19; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 21 Feb., 1586-7.
Hayward, Thomas, of the city of New Sarum, Wilts, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 24 July, 1584, aged 23.
Mills, Edward (Mylls), of Wilts, "cond. gen." Balliol Coll., matric. 30 Oct., 1584, aged 16.
Saunders, Edward, pleb. "com. Cannetriensis." Balliol Coll., matric. 21 Aug., 1584, aged 18.
Roberts, William, of co. Carnarvon, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 May, 1584, aged 19.
Walle, Nicholas, of co. Worcester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 9 Oct., 1584, aged 17.
Bentley, William, of co. Warwick, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 5 Oct., 1584, aged 14.
Story, Walter, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 30 Oct., 1584, aged 16.
Farr, Thomas, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1584, aged 14.
Powell, Robert, of co. Warwick, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 9 Oct. 1584, aged 16.
Starkye, Wake, of Northants, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 5 June, 1584, aged 18.
Brownell, Henry, of Notts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 17 July, 1584, aged 26.
Downe, Edward, of Wilts, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 24 July, 1584, aged 16.
Barnaby, John, of Wilts, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 9 Oct., 1584, aged 16.

Powel, Richard, of co. Warwick, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 29 Oct., 1585, aged 17; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 11 Feb., 1590-1; M.A. from Balliol Coll. 28 May, 1593; one of these names beneficed in Somersetshire. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Chalfont, Christopher, of Bucks, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 14; B.A. 14 Dec., 1590, M.A. 15 May, 1594, B.D. 7 July, 1603, licenced to preach 28 Jan., 1604-5.
Langford, Nicholas, of co. Worcester, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry under date 2 July, 1585, aged 12; B.A. from Christ Church 20 May, 1590, M.A. 30 May, 1593, proctor 1600.
Taylor, John, of Oxon, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 16, B.A. 28 June, 1593.
Gamble, John, "servus Dr. Lillie." Balliol Coll., matric. 29 Oct., 1585, B.A. 13 July, 1587.
Holland, George, of co. Worcester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 25 June, 1585, aged 15.
Head, Henry, of co. Leicester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 29 Oct., 1585, aged 22.
Colton, John (Coulton), 'servus Dr. Lillie.' Balliol Coll., matric. 29 Oct., 1585.
Robbins, John, of Somerset, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 17.
Lillye, Barnabye, "servus Dr. Lillie." Balliol Coll., matric. 29 Oct., 1585.
Hall, Thomas, of Salop, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 18.

Warren, Edward (Warine), of Northants, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 13 April, 1586, aged 18, B.A. sup. — May, 1589; M.A. 7 July, 1592; vicar of Duston, Northants, 1616, and of Marnham, Notts, 1627. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Michell, Edward (Mychell), of Sussex, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 21 June, 1586, aged 17; B.A. 27 May, 1590, student of Inner Temple 1592, as of Rudgwick, Sussex, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Dickson, George (Dicson), 'minister,' of Notts. Balliol Coll., matric. 13 April, 1586, aged 24.
Mogerege, George, of Wilts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 13 April, 1586, aged 16.

Gittins, Champion (Gyttyns), of Somerset, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1587, aged 17; B.A. 28 Jan., 1590-1, fellow, M.A. 6 Feb., 1593-4, licenced to preach 21 March, 1602-3, B.D. 7 July, 1603.
Noyes, Adam, of Wilts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 July, 1587, aged 16; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 10 May, 1593, rector of Woodborough, Wilts, 1597, father of John 1641. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wenman, Ferdinand (Wainman), of Bucks, arm. matric. entry 8 Dec., 1587, aged 12, "Mr. Case's scholar," B.A. from Balliol Coll. 10 April, 1592.
Keene, William, of Dorset, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 July, 1587, aged 14 (subs. as "Kine"), B.A. 26 Jan., 1591-2.
Mynores, Walter, of co. Derby, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 July, 1587, aged 18.
Floyd, John (Floide), Balliol Coll., matric. 12 July, 1587.

Popham, (Sir) Francis, of Somerset, arm. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 17 May, 1588, aged 15; of Houndstreet, Bath, and of Littlecote, Wilts, student of Middle Temple 1589 (as son and heir of John, of Wellington, Somerset, attorney-general to the Queen, and afterwards lord chief justice), knighted 1596, M.P. Somerset 1597-8, Wilts 1604-11, Marlborough 1614, Great Bedwin 1621-2, Chippenham 1624-5, 1625, 1626, 1628-9, Minehead 1640 (l.p.), until his death; buried at Stoke Newington 15 Aug., 1644, or at Mayors chapel, Bristol, 16 March, 1646-7; admon. 24 April, 1647; father of Alexander 1621. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Moore, Eustace, of co. Warwick, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 28 Feb., 1588-9, aged 16; B.A. 1 June, 1592, M.A. 26 June, 1595, licenced to preach 21 Nov., 1601, B.D. 22 Feb., 1604-5; rector of Denbury, Devon, 1605, and of Barwick, Somerset, 1606, canon of Worcester 1617; father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Collarde, Robert, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 17 May, 1588, aged 15, B.A. 26 Jan., 1591-2; M.A. from Lincoln Coll. 3 April, 1596, B.D. 12 April, 1605, prælector in Greek 1648, rector of Chilton Folliatt, Wilts, 1599. See Rawlinson, iii. 104; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Hanham, Sir John (Hanam), of Dorset, arm. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 17 May, 1588, aged 14; (s. Thomas, serjeant-at-law), knighted 11 May, 1603, high sheriff Dorset 1614, M. P. East Looe 1601, and Weymouth 1604-11; died unmarried. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Hele, John, of Devon, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 17 Jan., 1588-9, aged 17; a student of the Inner Temple 1590 (a son of John, a bencher of that inn), M.P. Plympton 1601, 1604-11; brother of Francis, etc., 1594. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Malet, John, of Somerset, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 17 May, 1588, aged 14; B.A. 28 Jan., 1590-1; perhaps of Enmore, Somerset; M.P. Bath 1624-5; died 10 April, 1644; brother of Gawen 1586. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Warre, Richard, of Somerset, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry 17 May, 1588, aged 14; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1598, as son and heir of Roger, esquire. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Yorke, William, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 17 May, 1588, aged 20, B.A. 2 May, 1592; of St. Martins parish, innholder; admon. at Oxford 26 March, 1618; brother of Stephen 1590.
Marshall, Thomas, of Essex, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 17 May, 1588, aged 18; student of Inner Temple 1591, as "late of Oxford University." See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Wilson, Robert, of co. Worcester, "Decani" fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 17 May, 1588, aged 16, B.A. 6 Feb., 1590-1, M.A. 15 May, 1599; brother of Thomas 1585.
Tanner, John, of Dorset, mercatoris fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 17 May, 1588, aged 18.
Carswell, William, of Somerset, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 17 May, 1588, aged 18.
Finche, Robert, of Bucks, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 28 Feb., 1588-9, aged 17.
Slade, Gregory, of Berks, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 17 May, 1588, aged 20.

Norris, William, of co. Leicester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 14 Nov., 1589, aged 18, B.A. 28 June, 1593; M.A. from Lincoln Coll. 19 June, 1601, vicar of Stonehouse, co. Gloucester, 1609; father of William 1636. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Apsley, Anthony, of Sussex, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1589, aged 18; of Horsham, sometime of Clifford's Inn, and a bar.-at-law of the Inner Temple 1598. See Berry's Sussex, 150; & Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Bonner, John, of London, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1589, aged 12; fellow of Exeter Coll. 1593, B.A. 4 Dec., 1596, died 5 March, 1596-7, son of William, of London, scrivener. See Boase, 51.
Yorke, Stephen, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry 6 Feb., 1589-90, aged 13; manciple of Brasenose Coll., admon. at Oxford 2 July, 1618; brother of William 1588.
Harrison, Thomas (Harryson), of co. Durham, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 2 May, 1589, aged 21; sup. for B.A. 17 July, 1596, M.A. 9 July, 1601; then in orders.
Cutler, Thomas, of London, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 6 Feb., 1589-90, aged 13; B.A. 7 Feb., 1593-4. See Henry 1589.
Cutler, Henry, of London, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1589, aged 15; B.A. 19 June, 1593. See Thomas, 1590.
Bennet, Edward (Bennytt), of co. Lincoln, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 21 Nov., 1589, aged 16; B.A. 28 June, 1593.
Freeman, Francis, of Bucks, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1589, aged 18.
Wyrley, John, of Northants, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1589, aged 16.
Bennet, Robert (Bennyt), pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 21 Nov., 1589, aged 14.
Yorke, John, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 6 Feb., 1589-90, aged 14.

Wakeman, Robert, of co. Worcester, cler. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. entry 24 July, 1590, aged 14, B.A. 6 Feb., 1593-4, chaplain, fellow 1596, M.A. 6 July, 1597, B.D. 22 Feb., 1604-5, supd. for licence to preach 3 Dec., 1606, D.D. 1 June, 1608; (son of Thomas, minister of Flyford Flavel, co. Worcester), canon of Exeter 1616, rector of Beer Ferris 1603, and of Charlton, Devon, 1610; buried in the chancel of Beer Ferris 19 Sept., 1629; father of Robert and Thomas 1627. See Ath. ii. 470; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Bradshaw, Nicholas (Bradshew), of Bucks, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 6 Nov., 1590, aged 15; fellow, B.A. 21 April, 1594, M.A. 31 March, 1599, B.D. 29 Nov., 1605, 'author of' "Canticvm Evangelicvm Summam Sacri Evangellii Continens," London, 1635, 8vo.,' rector of St. Mildred, Bread Street, 1604, and of Ockham, Surrey, 1607, father of Robert 1635. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Fitzjames, Leweston, of Dorset, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 5 March, 1590-1, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1594 (as son and heir of John, of Leweston, Dorset, esq.), M.P. Bridport 1597-8; buried at Long Burton, Dorset, 16 April, 1638; father of Henry, Thomas, and John 1636. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Hawkshurst, Samson (Hauxhurste), of Salop, cler. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 6 Nov., 1590, aged 19, B.A. 28 June, 1593; B.D. from Magdalen Hall 9 July, 1607, canon of Lichfield 1608-27, vicar of Nuneaton, co. Warwick, 1626-7. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Ma(i)nwaring, Robert, of Salop, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 6 Nov., 1590, aged 20; B.A. from St. John's Coll. 1 July, 1594, vicar of Winslow, Bucks, 1597-1648. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,671, p. 259.
Napper, Edward, of Oxon, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry 15 Oct., 1590, aged 12; student of Inner Temple 1600, as of Holywell, Oxon. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Story, John, of Somerset, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 6 Nov., 1590, aged 18; B.A. from BroadGates Hall 1 Feb., 1591-2. as Scory, which see.
Scory, John, of Somerset, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 6 Nov. 1590, aged 18; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 1 Feb., 1591-2.
King, William, of London, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry dated 5 March, 1590-1, aged 12.
Lapworth, Michael, of co. Warwick, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1590, aged 14.
Layer, William, of London, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 6 Nov., 1590, aged 17.

Claytonn, Thomas, of London, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1591, aged 16, B.A. 17 Oct., 1594; M.A. from Gloucester Hall 31 March, 1599, sup. for licence to practice medicine 27 March, 1610, B. & D.Med. 20 June, 1611, Regius professor of medicine 1612-47, principal of Broadgates Hall 1620-4, master of Pembroke College 1624, until his death 10 July, 1647, buried in Church of St. Aldate, Oxford.

Levinge, Timothy, co. Warwick, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 14 May, 1591, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1606, bencher 1622, serjeant-at-law 20 May, 1636, as of Baddesley Clinton, co. Warwick, sometime of Clement's Inn (4s. Thomas, of Parwich, Ashbourne, co. Derby), recorder of Derby, and M.P. 1621-2, 1624-5, 1625, and 1628-9; father of Thomas 1636. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Smith, Richard (Smyth), of Surrey, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 28 Jan., 1591-2, aged 17, B.A. 16 Jan., 1594-5, M.A. 31 March, 1599, fellow, B.D. 7 June, 1608, licenced to preach 26 Nov., 1608, D.D. supd. 11 Nov., 1611 (s. John, of Merrow, Surrey), rector of Shere, Surrey, 1613. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Ferrers, Edward, of co. Warwick, militis fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1591, aged 18; student of Middle Temple 1594 (as 2s. of Humphrey, of Tamworth, co. Warwick, kt.), perhaps M.P. Tewkesbury (April), 1610-11; brother of John 1583. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Ingram, Richard, of co. Worcester, arm, Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1591, aged 17; B.A. 17 Oct., 1594; M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 6 July, 1599; rector of Oddington-upon-Otmore, Oxon, 1614, and rector of Shenington, co. Gloucester, 1615. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Poole, Edward, of co. Derby, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 5 Nov., 1591, aged 15; B.A. 28 Feb., 1594-5 (as Pole), student of Middle Temple 1596, as 3s. German, of Radbourne, co. Derby, esq.; brother of Henry 1591. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Poole, Henry, of co. Derby, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 5 Nov., 1591, aged 14, B.A. 28 Feb., 1594-5, student of Middle Temple 1596, as 4s. German, of Radborne, co. Derby; brother of Edward 1591. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Trentham, Thomas, of co. Stafford, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 17; of Rossiter, co. Stafford, M.P. Newcastle-under-Lyme 1601, and perhaps knighted 9 April, 1627. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Smithe, Edward (Smythe), of Oxon, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. entry 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 14; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 8 Dec. 1596; perhaps vicar of Staunton Harcourt, Oxon, 1615. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Winter, Nathaniel, of Nottingham (town), pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 5 Nov., 1591, aged 16; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 5 Feb., 1596-7; M.A. from Gloucester Hall 10 July, 1601.
Hall, John (Haule), of co. Worcester, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 16 (? 18); B.A. 28 Feb., 1594-5; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 11 Nov., 1597.
Clavell, Giles, of co. Gloucester, 'cler. fil.' Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1591, aged 17, B.A. 16 Jan., 1594-5; M.A. from Gloucester Hall 23 June, 1598.
Mason, Robert, of Salop, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 5 Nov., 1591, aged 20; student of Lincoln's Inn 1597. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Littlewooke, Edmond, of Berks, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 13, B.A. sup. 27 Feb., 1600-1. See O.H.S. xii. 230.
Millwarde, Thomas, of co. Derby, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1591, aged 15; B.A. 28 Feb., 1594-5, M.A. 12 July, 1600.
Mundye, Thomas, of Wilts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1591, aged 16; B.A. 28 Feb., 1594-5, M.A. sup. 19 July, 1600.
Mayo, Henry, of co. Hereford, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 28 Jan., 1591-2, aged 18; B.A. 14 Nov., 1600.
Fisher, Edward, of Berks, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1591, aged 14; B.A. 6 Feb., 1593-4.
Parvishe, George, of London, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. entry 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 11.
Johnsonne, John, of co. Worcester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1591, aged 16.
Leighe, Henry, of co. Derby, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 5 Nov., 1591, aged 17.

Coventry, Thomas, of co. Worcester, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 14; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1603 (as son and heir of Thomas, of Hill Croome), bencher 1614, autumn reader 1616, recorder of London 1616, solicitor-general 1617-21, attorney-general 1621, and knighted 15 March, 1617, M.P. Droitwich 1621-2, lord keeper of the great seal 1625, created Lord Coventry of Aylesbury 10 April, 1628, died 14 Jan., 1639-40, father of Francis, Henry, and William. See Ath., ii. 650; Foster's Judges and Barristers; & D.N.B.

Kinge, Richard, of co. Hereford, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 19; B.A. 28 Nov., 1595, M.A. 9 July, 1601 (then in orders), B. and D.D. 20 June, 1611, vicar of Newbold-onAvon, co. Warwick, 1604-11, rector of Toppesfield 1605, vicar of Great Bentley 1612, and of Kelvedon (all Essex), 1613. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Marler, Toby, of Somerset, cler. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 20; B.A. 5 July, 1595 vicar of Creech St. Michael, Somerset, 1627, until his death 1641; he succeeded on the death of his father (David who had been vicar 1565-1627). See Foster's Index Eccl.

Coles, Thomas, of Wilts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 17; B.A. 19 Feb., 1595-6, rector of Fifield Bavant, Wilts, 1606, preb. of Hill Deverell, in Heytesbury, 1611, perhaps father of the next, and of William 1635. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Wyndham, Edmund (Windham), of Somerset, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1603; perhaps brother of George 1614, Henry 1599, John and Thomas 1600, Hugh 1642, and Zacharias 1596. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Michell, John, of Sussex, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 Dec., 1592, aged 16; perhaps a student of Gray's Inn 1583 (as son of Thomas, of Anstey, Sussex, esq.); one of these names a master in chancery 1619-44. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Clintone, Guy, of Wilts, 'cler. fil.' Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 16; B.A. 28 Nov., 1595, M.A. 6 July, 1599, rector of Alford, Somerset, 1611, father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Poyer, Richard, of Somerset, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 22, B.A. 21 Feb., 1595-6; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 4 July, 1601, then in orders.
Hawkins, Samuel (Haukins), of Berks, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 15; B.A. 6 Feb., 1593-4, M.A. 7 July, 1598.
Hankyns, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 Dec., 1592, aged 16.
Lee, George, of Devon, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 15.

Lapthorne, Anthony, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 19 June, 1593; fellow Exeter Coll. 1593-1600, M.A. 8 July, 1596, chaplain to James I., vicar of Lanrake, Cornwall, 1600, rector of Minchinhampton, co. Gloucester, 1612, and of Tretire, co. Hereford, until deprived 9 Oct., 1634, settled rector of Sedgefield, co. Durham, May, 1647, by the Westminster assembly. See Add. MS. 15,671, p. 244; Boase, 51; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Littleton, (Sir) Edward, of co. Stafford, militis fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 17; student of Inner Temple 1595 (as son and heir of Sir Edward, of Pillaton Hall, co. Stafford, gen.), knighted 22 Aug., 1621, M.P. co. Stafford, 1624-5; brother of Thomas 1594, and father of Edward 1617, and of Walter 1626. See Foster's Inns of Court Register.

Abbott, John, of Surrey, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 17; B.A. 17 June, 1597, M.A. 26 March, 1601, licence to preach 1 Feb., 1607-8, B.D. 25 June, 1608, D.D. 27 Jan., 1612-13, preb. of Canterbury 1613, rector of Great Chart, Kent, 1613, buried 1 Sept., 1615. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Baynham, Edmund (Beynham), of co. Cambridge, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 16; B.A. 20 Feb., 1595-6, query a student of the Middle Temple 1596, as son and heir of Wm., of Boxley, Kent, esquire, and M.P. Bishops Castle 1597-8. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Stoughton, Thomas, of Surrey, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 14, B.A. 17 June, 1597; (son of Sir Lawrence), born 19 Aug., 1580; student of Inner Temple 1598, as of Stoke, near Guildford, Surrey, gent.; died 22 March, 1610-11. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Stoughton, Lawrence, of Surrey, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 16; student of Inner Temple 1595, as of Stoughton, Surrey (s. Sir Laurence); born 4 Feb., 1577-8, died 22 Aug., 1597. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Parks, William, of London, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 17; B.A. 15 April, 1597, M.A. 12 July, 1600, scholar Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, June, 1592, vicar of Whiteparish, Wilts, 1604. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Worthe, John, of Somerset, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 20, B.A. 21 Oct., 1597, M.A. from Gloucester Hall 31 May, 1600; rector of High Ham, Somerset, 1599. See Foster's Index Eccl.
French, Mathew, of Northants, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 17; B.A. 4 Feb., 1596-7, rector of North Meols, co. Lancaster, 1600. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Knight, John, of Hants, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 18; student of Inner Temple 1595, as of St. Denis, Hants. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Irelande, Robert, of Salop, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 19; student of Lincoln's Inn 1595. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Morgann, Curtes, of Wilts, cler. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. entry 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 13; B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 2 May, 1599, as Morgan.
Atkins, John, of Somerset, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 6 April, 1593, aged 18; B.A. 21 Feb., 1595-6.
Tilson, John (Tylsone), of co. Lincoln, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 22 March, 1593-4, aged 18.
Kirton, Joseph (Kyrton), of Somerset, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 1. Feb., 1593-4, aged 20.
Blythe, Edmund (Blithe), of Oxon, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Feb., 1593-4, aged 25.
Lathbury, Francis, of co. Derby, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 19.
Giles, John (Gyles), of London, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 17.
Fisher, Jeffrey, of Wilts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Feb., 1593-4, aged 24.
Batson, Henry, of Wilts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Feb., 1593-4, aged 28.

Gresley, (Sir) George, of co. Derby. Balliol Coll., matric. Nov., 1594, aged 14, as arm.; student of Inner Temple 1598, as of Drakelow, co. Derby (s. Sir Thomas), said to have been knighted, created a baronet 29 June, 1611, M.P. Newcastle-under-Lyne 1628-9; buried in the Temple church 5 Feb., 1650-1; brother of Henry 1594, and John 1598, and of Walsingham and William. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Washington, (Sir) Laurence, of Herts, gen. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. — Nov., 1594, aged 15; of Westbury, and of Garsdon, Bucks, a student of Gray's Inn 1607 (as son and heir of Laurence, of Maidstone), succeeded his father as registrar court of chancery 1619, knighted 29 July, 1627; buried 24 May, 1643, will dated 11th, and proved 23rd same month.

Hele, (Sir) Francis, of Devon, arm. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1594, aged 17; a student of the Inner Temple 1594 (as son of John, serjt.-atlaw, elect), knighted 1 Nov., 1608; died 27 March, 1623; brother of John 1589, of George 1602. of Nicholas 1601, and perhaps of Thomas 1598. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Yeaman, William (Yeeman), of Bristol, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 16, B.A. 4 May, 1598; M.A. from Gloucester Hall 10 July, 1601 (as Yemans); vicar of Bristol St. Philip 1603, prebendary of Bristol 1622. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Fowler, Bryan, of co. Stafford, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 14; student of Inner Temple 1598, as of the manor of St. Thomas, co. Stafford (s. Walter); died at Naples unmarried. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Wyrley, William (Wurley), of Stafford, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1594, aged 29, son of Augustine of Nether Seale, co. Leicester, rouge croix pursuivant of arms 1604, until his death in Feb., 1617. See Ath. ii. 217.

Tasbrough, John, of Suffolk, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. — Nov. 1594, aged 18; student of Lincoln's Inn 1595, as of Bucks (Beaconsfield), perhaps M.P. Wycombe 1597-8, and knighted 11 May, 1603. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Littleton, Thomas, of co. Stafford, equitis fil. Balliol Coll., matric. Nov., 1594, aged 16; student of Inner Temple 1598, as of Pillaton Hall, co. Stafford, gent.; brother of Edward 1594. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Wellys, John, of co. Stafford, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. — Nov., 1594, aged 18; student of Gray's Inn 1598, as son and heir of Humphrey, of Hoarcross, co. Stafford, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Gresley, Henry, of co. Derby, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. (? 22) Nov., 1594, aged 15; sup. for B.A. 27 May, 1597 (1s. Sir Thomas); died s.p.; brother of George same date, and of John 1598.
Downe, Jerome, of Wilts, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 June, 1594, aged 18; of the Middle Temple 1594, as 2nd son of Nicholas, of Orcheston, Wilts, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Ellis, Andrew, of Bristol, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. Nov. 1594, aged 19; perhaps B.A. from St. Mary Hall 11 Feb., 1601-2.
Cowper, Richard, of London, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 7 June, 1594, aged 16; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 17 Feb., 1598-9.
Debancke, Edward (Deabancke), of Oxon, 'cler. fil.' Balliol Coll., matric. 5 July, 1594, aged 16. See Robert 1585.
Hungerford, Thomas, of Wilts, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 7 June, 1594, aged 16, B.A. 29 Jan., 1596-7.
Bellamy, John (Bellymye), of Somerset, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1594, aged 17.
Holland, John, of co. Denbigh, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 28 June, 1594, aged 15.
Osborne, John, of Bucks, cler. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 7 June, 1594, aged 16.
Fleminge, Philip, of Wilts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 28 June, 1594, aged 19.
Smith, John, of Sussex, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 3 May, 1594, aged 17.
Cole, Edward, of Salop, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. Nov., 1594, aged 18.

Saunders, Cullingwood, of co. Derby, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1595, aged 17; student of Inner Temple 1598, as Collingwood, of Lullington, co. Derby, gent. (s. Thomas); born 28 Aug., 1578; died 6 May, 1653. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Farmor, (Sir) Hatton, of Northants, militis fil. nat. max. Balliol Coll., matric. 9 May, 1595, aged 15; (1s. Sir George) knighted at Easton 9 July, 1603; buried 28 Oct., 1640; brother of George 1603, and father of Hatton 1640, and William, 1636.

Talbot, Thomas, of Wilts, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 21 Nov., 1595, aged 15; perhaps a student of Gray's Inn 1599 (as 3s. John, of Salwarp, co. Worcester); perhaps of Oakeley Hall in Salwarp; brother of John same date.

Colston, George, of Bristol, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 31 Oct., 1595, aged 14; B.A. 4 May, 1598, of the Middle Temple 1599, as son and heir of William, of Bristol, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Jackman, Edward, of Essex, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 21 Nov., 1595, aged 18; student of Lincoln's Inn 1599. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Scorey, Edmund (Skorye), of co. Hereford, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1595, aged 20; son of Silvanus Scory. See Ath. ii. 89, 770.
Bibie, Thomas (Bybie), of Essex, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 28 Nov., 1595, aged 17; B.A. 7 Dec., 1598, M.A. 9 July, 1601.
Talbot, John, of Wilts, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 21 Nov., 1595, aged 16; brother of Thomas same date.
Paultock, John, of Surrey, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 31 Oct., 1595, aged 15; B.A. 12 June, 1599.
Ingram, John (Ingraham), of co. Worcester, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 21 Nov., 1595, aged 19.
Milles, Edward (Mylles), of Wilts, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 12 March, 1595-6, aged 21.
Thomas, Aquila, of Somerset, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1595, aged 23.
Archere, Adam, of Wilts, armiger. Balliol Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1595, aged 18.
Whitton, Whorwood, of Salop, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 21 Nov., 1595, aged 17.
Barker, William, of Salop, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 21 Nov., 1595, aged 18.

Blancheard, Thomas, of Winchester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 12 Nov., 1596, aged 16; B.A. 31 May, 1600, M.A. 27 March, 1604, B.D. 7 June, 1613, rector of North Wraxall, Wilts, 1613, and of Cold Ashton, co. Gloucester, 1624, father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Smithe, George, of co. Warwick, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1596-7, aged 16, B.A. 23 June, 1601; student of Gray's Inn 1595, as 2s. George (s. Francis), of Wootten Wawen, co. Warwick, esq. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Fisher, Robert, of co. Warwick, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 14 Jan., 1596-7, aged 18; perhaps son of Sir Clement, of Packington, and himself created a bart. 7 Dec., 1622; died 29 March, 1647.
Thacker, Godfrey, of co. Derby, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. entry 25 June, 1596, aged 14; perhaps bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1608, as Geoffrey. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Woodward, Richard, of co. Warwick, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 25 June, 1596, aged 17; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 18 June, 1599.
Cratford, Charles, of co. Worcester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1596, aged 18; B.A. 31 May, 1600.
Bowyer, Edmund, of Somerset, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 3 April, 1596, aged 19, see John same date.
Bowyer, John, of Somerset, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 3 April, 1596, aged 17; see Edmund same date.
Fleete, Thomas, of co. Worcester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 23 July, 1596, aged 17.
Ashfeilde, Thomas, of Oxon, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 23 July, 1596, aged 18.
Goddard, Vincent, of Beds, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 3 April, 1596, aged 15.
Wilson, William, of Yorks, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 23 July, 1596, aged 19.

Tillesley, Richard, of co. Warwick, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 15; scholar St. John's Coll. 5 July, 1603, M.A. 26 June, 1607, B.D. 22 Nov., 1613, licenced to preach 25 Nov., 1613, D.D. 7 July, 1617 (son of Thomas, of Eccleshall, co. Stafford); rector of Cuxton 1614, and Stone, Kent, 1615, and archdeacon 1614, and canon of Rochester 1615; buried in the cathedral 1621. See Ath. ii. 303; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Boswell, William, of Bristol, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 16; B.A. 1 July, 1601, M.A. 16 Dec., 1605, B.D. 7 July, 1618, vicar of St. Laurence, Old Jewry, London, 1616, rector of Horton, Bucks, 1618, licenced 9 Feb., 1619-20, to marry Frances, daughter of Thomas Waller, late of Beaconsfield, Bucks, esq., deceased. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Goodridge, John, of Devon, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 17, B.A. 13 Feb., 1601-2; M.A. from Gloucester Hall 30 June, 1606, fellow Wadham Coll. 1613, B.Med. 30 Jan., 1618-19, licenced to practice medicine 4 Feb., 1618-19, Gresham professor of rhetoric 1638, warden of Lord Northampton's (Trinity) hospital at Greenwich, where he died Nov., 1624. See Gardiner, 4.

Horton, Christopher, of co. Derby, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 14; B.A. 14 Nov., 1600, student of Middle Temple 1602 (as son and heir of Walter, of Catton, co. Derby, esq.); high sheriff co. Derby, 1640; died 18 Nov., 1659, aged 77; father of the next-named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Higges, William, of co. Derby, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 27 Jan., 1597-8, aged 17, B.A. 21 Feb., 1600-1; perhaps vicar of Little Birch, co. Hereford, 1611; father of the last-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Brunsell, Oliver, of Wilts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 15; B.A. 8 July, 1603, vicar of Elingdon alias Wroughton, Wilts, 1613, father of Henry. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Tayler, Thomas, of co. Worcester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 17, B.A. 11 July, 1600; rector of Leigh, co. Worcester, 1605. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Harcourt, Michael (Harecourt), of Oxon, equitis fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 18; captain of a ship under Sir Walter Raleigh; brother of Robert 1590.
Gorton, John, of Somerset, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 19; possibly a student of Gray's Inn 1605. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Williames, Hugh, of Flints, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 11 May, 1597, aged 20; perhaps B.A. from St. Mary Hall 21 May, 1600.
Edwardes, Christopher, of Oxon, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 18; B.A. 1 July, 1601, M.A. 16 May, 1605.
Gresley, John, of co. Derby, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 16; brother of George and Henry 1594.
Keene, Robert, of Bucks, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 16; B.A. 11 July, 1600, M.A. 5 July, 1605.
Leigh, Edward, of co. Warwick, equitis fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 16. See also Thomas 1608.
Sturley, Richard, of co. Worcester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 6 May, 1597, aged 16, B.A. 23 March, 1600-1.
Ston(e)banck, Ralph, of Bucks, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 16, B.A. 5 March, 1602-3.
Lenthrop, Peter, of London, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 18; B.A. 5 March 1601-2.
Bushell, Henry, of co. Gloucester, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 16.
Collins, Thomas, of co. Worcester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 17.
Tynney, Nathaniel, of co. Warwick, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 18.
Eyton, Thomas, of co. Hereford, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 19.
Gortonn, George, of Somerset, pleb. Balliol Coll. matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 20.
Vaughan, William, of Flints, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 16.
Parsonn, John, of co. Warwick, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 6 May, 1597, aged 15.

Baker, John, of co. Worcester, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 5 May, 1598, aged 13. (Memo.: John, son and heir of Francis, of Hillcourt, Grafton Flyford, co. Worcester, gent., bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1607.) See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Baxter, George, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 Dec., 1598, aged 16; B.A. 3 July, 1602, M.A. 6 July, 1605, rector of Little Wenlock, Salop, 1608, father of Stephen. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Orgell, Richard, of co. Stafford, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 26 Jan., 1598-9, aged 15; B.A. 16 Feb., 1603-4, M.A. 23 April, 1607, rector of Newton Regis, co. Warwick, 1617. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Heveningham, Nicholas, of co. Stafford, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 26 Jan., 1598-9, aged 14; probably father of the last-named.
Knight, George, of co. Worcester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 Dec., 1598, aged 16; B.A. 14 July, 1602, M.A. 12 June, 1605.

fore: George (? Rewill, Rewitt, Revell, or Kevell)
txt: Revitt, George (? Rewill, Rewitt, Revell, or Kevell), of co. Warwick, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 28 April, 1598, aged 18.
Brittin, Thomas, of Somerset, 'cler. fil.' Balliol Coll., matric. 30 June, 1598, aged 18; B.A. 5 March, 1602-3.

Tilson, Henry, s. Henry, born at Midgley, baptised at Halifax 13 Oct., 1577; B.A. from Balliol Coll. 29 Jan., 1596-7, fellow University Coll. 1599, M.A. 1 July, 1601; rector of Stanmer, Sussex, 1614-15, vicar of Rochdale, co. Lancaster, 1615-35, dean of Christ Church, Dublin, 1634-9, prebendary St. Patrick's Cathedral, Dublin, 1635, archdeacon of Connor 1636, bishop of Elphin 1639, fled to England on the insurrection in 1645, and settled at Soothill Hall, Yorkshire, and died there 31 March, 1655, buried at Dewsbury 2 April. See Chetham, i. N.S. 81; Ath. iv. 814; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Gulliford, Brent, of Kent, cler. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 13 July, 1599, aged 15; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 9 July, 1603, M.A. 1 April, 1607, vicar of Congresbury, Somerset (on the death of Robert Gulliford, B.D.), 2 April, 1613, until his death shortly before 23 Aug., 1614. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Chadwell, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 20; B.A. 14 July, 1602, of the Middle Temple 1604, as son and heir of William, of Broadwell, co. Gloucester. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Norris, Edward, of co. Gloucester, cler. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 30 March, 1599, aged 15; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 23 Jan., 1606-7, M.A. 25 Oct., 1609, rector of Anmer, Norfolk, 1624. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lawrence, Ellis, of Dorset, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1599, aged 15; B.A. from Hart Hall 6 May, 1603, rector of Semley, Wilts, 1628. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Coyne, Thomas, of co. Stafford, 'arm. fil. nat. max.' Balliol Coll., matric. 29 Feb., 1599-1600, aged 15; called Coyney in Mat. Reg.
Blackwall, Richard, of co. Derby, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1599, aged 15; B.A. 10 May, 1604, M.A., 3 July, 1607.
Merrye, Richard, of Northants, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 13 July, 1599, aged 16; B.A. 14 Feb., 1602-3, M.A. 8 Dec., 1605.
Cotton, Francis, s. (Henry), bishop of Salisbury. Balliol Coll., matric. 24 May, 1599, aged 15; brother of Henry 1605.
Comberford, Henry, of co. Stafford, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 29 Feb., 1599-1600, aged 12.
Coulstocke, Thomas, of Sussex, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1599, aged 16.
Hill, Edward, of co. Warwick, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 24 May, 1599, aged 16.
Goddard, William, of Wilts, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 24 May, 1599, aged 17.

Russe, John, subscribed 1 Jan., 1595-6; B.A. from Balliol Coll. 31 Jan., 1598-9; fellow Oriel Coll. 1600, from Northants, M.A. 27 March, 1604, Bodley's librarian 1620-52 (as Rouse), a friend of Milton; buried in the college chapel April, 1652. See Gutch, i. 135; & Burrows, 536.

Holloway, Thomas, of co. Worcester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 4 April, 1600, aged 15; B.A. 17 Dec., 1603, M.A. 5 July, 1606, B.D. 7 June, 1613, licenced to preach 14 Feb., 1613-14, D.D. 15 May, 1616, vicar of St. Lawrence, Old Jewry, London. See Newcourt.
Woodford, John, of London, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 4 April, 1600, aged 15; B.A. from Christ Church 19 Jan., 1603-4, M.A. 30 Oct., 1606. See St. Paul's S. R. 30.
Price, Timothy, of Salop, cler. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 4 July, 1600, aged 15.

Brabazon, Wallop, of co. Warwick, eq. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 15; student of the Middle Temple 1604 (as 3rd son of Edward, of Whittaker, co. Warwick, knight); of Eaton, co. Hereford, high sheriff 1630, father of Edward and brother of Henry last named. See Foster's Peerage, E. Meath; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Goddard, Vincent, of Wilts, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 May, 1601, aged 15; demy Magdalen Coll. 1601-5, B.A. 12 Dec., 1604, fellow 1605-35, M.A. 8 July, 1607, proctor 1613 (s. William of Ogbourne St. Andrew, Wilts, gen.); baptised 14 Nov., 1585. See Bloxam, v. 3.

Mosse, John, of Northants, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 13 Nov., 1601, aged 17; B.A. 16 April, 1605, M.A. 14 Oct., 1608, vicar of Quadring, co. Lincoln, 1606, rector of Hope All Saints, Kent, 1608, and of Digswell, Herts, 1612. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Barker, Thomas, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 4 Dec., 1601, aged 18; B.A. 20 Oct., 1607, rector of Llandogget, co. Denbigh, 1611, vicar choral St. Asaph 1617, rector of Denbigh 1624. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Hanbury, Edward, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 19 June, 1601, aged 18; possibly bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1615, as 6s. of Walter, late of Benehall, co. Worcester, gent., deceased. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Maddacks, Richard, of co. Stafford, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 26 June, 1601, aged 16; B.A. 7 Feb., 1604-5, M.A. 11 Dec., 1607; one of these names rector of Pleasley, co. Derby, 1613. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Morley, Caleb, of Surrey, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 5 March, 1601-2, aged 16; B.A. 26 Oct., 1605, M.A. 20 June, 1611, rector of Stalbridge, Dorset, 1616. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hooper, Thomas, of Dorset, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 4 Dec., 1601, aged 20; B.A. 6 June, 1605; student of Lincoln's Inn 1606. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Blackwell, Robert (Blakwell), of co. Stafford, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 3 July, 1601, aged 14. See Foster's Gray's Inn Admissions.
Brabazon, Henry, of co. Warwick, eq. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 15; died young, brother of Wallop next named.
Aylworth, Thomas, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 19 June, 1601, aged 16. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Keynell, Richard, of Dorset, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 May, 1601, aged 16; B.A. 16 April, 1605.
Haviland, Edward, of co. Gloucester, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 May, 1601, aged 15.
Phill, Edward, of co. Gloucester pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 13 Nov., 1601, aged 18.
Woolley, Thomas, of co. Warwick, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 18.
Burchall, Richard, of Wilts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 5 March, 1601-2, aged 17.
Windowe, John, of Dorset, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 18.

Hoode, Paul, of co. Leicester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 19 Nov., 1602, aged 16; B.A. 31 Oct., 1606, M.A. 8 Dec., 1609 (incorporated at Cambridge 1614), B.D. from Lincoln Coll. 13 Dec., 1617, licenced to preach 20 July, 1619, D.D. 14 Oct., 1623, rector of his college 1620-68, vice-chancellor 1660, rector of Broughton Gifford, Wilts, 1621, of Kettesby, co. Lincoln, 1630, of Eyton, Northants, 1631-49, of Ickford, Bucks, 1660, canon of Southwell 1661-2; died 2 Aug., 1668, aged 84. See Burrows, 507; Foster's Index Eccl.; & Add. MS. 15,670, p. 96.

Tidmershe, John, of co. Worcester, cler. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 11 June, 1602, aged 16, B.A. 26 Oct., 1605, M.A. 25 June, 1608; vicar of Alderminster, co. Worcester, 1620; one of these names, probably his father, rector of Belbroughton, co. Worcester, 1584, and of Broadwell, co. Gloucester, 1598. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hall, Richard, of co. Worcester, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 11 June, 1602, aged 16; B.A. 27 Nov., 1604, M.A. 3 July, 1607, [perhaps B.D. from All Souls' Coll. 19 May, 1617; (incorporated at Cambridge 1617), D.D. 18 Dec., 1620]; perhaps rector of Beverstone with Kingscote, co. Gloucester, 1617. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Vernon, (Sir) Edward, of co. Stafford, arm. fil. nat. max. Balliol Coll., matric. 19 Nov., 1602, aged 17, B.A. 31 Oct., 1606, M.A. 20 June, 1609; of Houndshill, co. Stafford (1s. Walter), born 14 Dec., 1584, knighted 18 Aug., 1624; died 15 June, 1657; father of Edward 1640; brother of Thomas and Walter 1604.

Willoughby, (Sir) Rotheram, of Bucks, arm. fil. nat. max. Balliol Coll., matric. entry 4 June, 1602, aged 12, B.A. 23 April, 1607 (as "eq. fil. n. m."); of Carlton, Notts, knighted 24 May, 1608; brother of John and William 1609, and father of William 1624.

Cartwright, Nicholas (Cartrighte), of co. Worcester, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 11 June, 1602, aged 18; B.A. 16 Dec., 1605, M.A. 25 June, 1608, rector of Bourton-on-the-Hill, co. Gloucester, 1617. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

West, Christopher, of Devon, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1602, aged 17, B.A. 20 May, 1606, M.A. 20 June, 1609; incorporated at Cambridge 1614; rector of Willingham All Saints, Suffolk, 1619. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Wallis, Robert, of Bucks, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 11 Feb., 1602-3, aged 16, B.A. 28 Jan., 1607-8, M.A. 5 July, 1611; rector of Mursley 1622, and of Ellesborough, (both) Bucks, 1635. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Caulfeild, Thomas (Callfeilde), of Oxon, arm. Balliol Coll., matric. 18 June, 1602, aged 17, B.A. 23 June, 1602; M.A. from Gloucester Hall 30 June, 1606, probably brother of Alexander.
Wilkinson, Thomas, of co. Worcester, cler. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 28 Jan., 1602-3, aged 15, B.A. 5 July, 1606, M.A. 8 Dec., 1609.
Turner, Richard, of co. Gloucester, gen. fil. nat. max. Balliol Coll., matric. 11 June, 1602, aged 18.
Wilkinson, William, of Bucks, arm. fil. nat. max. Balliol Coll., matric. entry 4 June, 1602, aged 14.
Holcombe, John (Holtum), of co. Warwick, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1602, aged 18.
Allen, William, of co. Derby, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 19 Nov., 1602, aged 18.
Otten, Mathias, of London, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 18 June, 1602, aged 15.
Stokes, Thomas, of Oxon, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 19 Nov., 1602, aged 16.

Oglander, (Sir) John, of Southants (i.e. Isle of Wight), arm. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 July, 1603, aged 18; of Nunwell, Isle of Wight, student of Middle Temple 1604 (as son and heir of William, of West Deane, Sussex, esq.); deputy-governor of Portsmouth and of the Isle of Wight, knighted 22 Dec., 1615; M.P. Yarmouth (Isle of Wight) 1625, 1626, and 1628-9; died 28 Nov., 1655; father of George and John. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Marriott, Thomas, of Northants, gent. Balliol Coll., matric. 17 Feb., 1603-4, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1608 (as 2nd son Anthony, of Ashton, Northants, gent.), vicar of Daventry, Northants; died 21 March, 1615-16; buried there next day. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Foster's Index Eccl.

Farmer, George, of Northants., militis fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 8 July, 1603, aged 16; a student of the Middle Temple 1607 (as 3s. of George, of Easton Neston, Northants, knt.), buried there 25 Feb., 1634-5; brother of Hatton 1595. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Goughe, Robert, of Wilts, pleb. Balliol Coll., matric. 15 April, 1603, aged 19; B.A. 1 March, 1605-6 (perhaps eldest son of Hugh 1560), chaunter of Limerick cathedral, etc., 1615, archdeacon of Ardfert 1628; made his will 24 March, 1640; brother of William 1603.

Holgreve, George, of Devon, cler. fil. Balliol Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 18; rector of Woolfardisworthy, Devon, 1615, succeeded another George who compounded for the first-fruits 13 May, 1583. See Foster's Index Eccl.
